description: Hard-fail recall on Ollama down (with --allow-degraded opt-out) and add cross-encoder reranking via gpustack/llama-box for free-text --query recall. Includes Dockerfiles + docker-compose for both services.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test` (per-iteration: scoped to touched modules)
No breaking changes to public `recall_learnings()` / `recall_learnings_with_backend()` signatures
No `unwrap()` in production code paths — use `map_err`/`?` for proper error propagation
RetrievalBackend trait remains recall-policy-agnostic (no `allow_degraded` field on RetrievalQuery)
Reranker is opt-in: when `rerankerUrl` is unset, recall behavior is unchanged from today
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio); we are pre-launch, foundations compound
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — qualityDimensions on every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Phase 2 foundation — prefer solutions that lay strong post-launch foundations (1:10+ savings ratio)
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at REVIEW-001 — REVIEW must leave the trunk green including pre-existing failures
Ship working code with tests to prove it; handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)
Abstractions with only one concrete use that aren't justified by test injection
Error messages that don't identify which service is down or what to fix
Catch-all error handlers that swallow context
Reranker silently producing different results than requested model (no model-name verification)
Backward-incompatible changes to existing recall_learnings() public signature

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `cargo run -- recall --query "x"` with Ollama stopped exits non-zero and prints the OllamaUnreachable message containing all three actionable hints (`ollama serve`, `ollama pull`, `--allow-degraded`).
Positive: same command with `--allow-degraded` exits 0 and returns FTS5/patterns results (vector returns empty, no error propagated).
Positive: `recall --for-task TASK-ID` with Ollama stopped exits 0 (no Ollama call attempted because no query text).
Negative: `RetrievalQuery` struct does NOT contain an `allow_degraded` field. Grep `git diff` for it inside RetrievalQuery's definition — must be zero hits.
Negative: `recall_learnings()` legacy entry point signature unchanged (no new required parameters).

Unit test: `VectorBackend::default().with_strict_mode(true)` pointing at `127.0.0.1:0` with non-empty query text → `Err(TaskMgrError::OllamaUnreachable { url, model, .. })` with `url == "http://127.0.0.1:0"`.
Unit test: same backend with `with_strict_mode(false)` → `Ok(empty)`.
Unit test: empty query text (`Some("")`) with `strict=true` → `Ok(empty)` (no Ollama call attempted).
Unit test: clap parses `task-mgr recall --query x --allow-degraded` and round-trips `allow_degraded=true`.

Known-bad: an implementation that calls `embedder.embed("")` and then errors on the empty input would pass 'Ollama down test' but fail the 'empty query short-circuits' test. Must short-circuit BEFORE the HTTP call.
Known-bad: an implementation that propagates strict mode through `RetrievalQuery` would compile but pollute the trait. Test must inspect `RetrievalQuery`'s field list and assert no `allow_degraded`.
Failure mode: if Ollama returns valid HTTP but malformed JSON in strict mode → `Err(OllamaUnreachable)` with the underlying IoError as `source`, not a panic.
CONTRACT: `OllamaUnreachable.url` matches `OllamaEmbedder.base_url` exactly (verify by grepping `src/learnings/embeddings/mod.rs:25-50` for the field name and reusing it).
userStories.description:
Wire hard-fail-on-Ollama-down through the entire recall path with `--allow-degraded` as the offline-use opt-out.

DO:
- Add `OllamaUnreachable { url: String, model: String, source: std::io::Error }` to `TaskMgrError` (src/error.rs) with a `Display` impl that produces actionable hints: 'Is Ollama running? Try: ollama serve', 'Is the model pulled? Try: ollama pull <model>', 'To run recall without semantic search, pass --allow-degraded'.
- Add `strict: bool` field to `VectorBackend` (src/learnings/retrieval/vector.rs:41-54). Add `with_strict_mode(self, bool) -> Self` builder method. Default is `strict=false` to preserve existing callers (`CompositeBackend::default_backends`, `recall_learnings`).
- In `VectorBackend::retrieve` at lines 79-82 and 86-89: when `strict=true`, propagate embed/load errors as `TaskMgrError::OllamaUnreachable` (use the embedder's URL+model fields). When `strict=false`, keep current `Ok(Vec::new())` swallow behavior.
- Extend `CompositeBackend::with_ollama_config` (composite.rs:34-42) to accept `strict: bool` and construct `VectorBackend::new(...).with_strict_mode(strict)`.
- Add `allow_degraded: bool` to `RecallParams` (src/learnings/mod.rs / src/learnings/recall/mod.rs:31-46). Do NOT add to `RetrievalQuery`.
- Add `allow_degraded: bool` to `RecallCmdParams` (src/commands/recall.rs:18-35). At line 131, build `CompositeBackend::with_ollama_config(url, model, !allow_degraded)`.
- Add `#[arg(long = "allow-degraded")] allow_degraded: bool` to `Commands::Recall` in src/cli/commands.rs:467-492.
- Wire from CLI through main.rs:440-471 into `RecallCmdParams`.
- Tests for both strict (errors out) and non-strict (returns empty) paths in vector.rs's existing test module.
- Test that `--allow-degraded` parses correctly in src/cli/tests.rs.

DO NOT:
- Add `allow_degraded` to `RetrievalQuery` — backends are recall-policy-agnostic.
- Change `recall_learnings()` legacy entry point's behavior — it stays non-strict for back-compat.
- Use `unwrap()` on the embedder error — wrap in `OllamaUnreachable`.

Edge cases:
- Empty `--query ""`: clap treats it as `Some("")`. Vector backend already handles via `Some(t) if !t.is_empty()` guard at vector.rs:73-75 — empty string returns Ok(empty) without an Ollama call. Strict mode should NOT fire here (no embedding attempted).
- `--for-task` only (no query text): vector backend never embeds. No Ollama call. Strict mode does nothing. Behavior unchanged.
- Ollama up but returns invalid JSON: parse error in `embed()` propagates as `OllamaUnreachable` source (use the IoError-wrapped variant — single error type per locked decision).

Known-bad to guard against:
- `--allow-degraded` accidentally affecting `--for-task` recall (which doesn't use the embedder). Test must pass both flags together and verify no behavioral change.
- Constructing `OllamaUnreachable` with the *configured default* URL when the user has set a custom one in config.json. Use the embedder's actual URL field.
edgeCases: Empty query string `--query ""`: short-circuit before Ollama call regardless of strict mode (current behavior at vector.rs:73-75)
`--for-task` only (no query text): no Ollama call attempted; strict mode is inert
Ollama up but returns malformed JSON in strict mode: propagate as OllamaUnreachable with the parse error as source
`--allow-degraded` passed but Ollama is actually up: behavior unchanged from non-degraded path (vector results included)

failureModes.cause: Ollama HTTP connect refused in strict mode
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Err(OllamaUnreachable { url, model, source: ConnectionRefused }) propagated to CLI; non-zero exit

failureModes.cause: Ollama HTTP timeout in strict mode
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Err(OllamaUnreachable) with the timeout IoError as source

failureModes.cause: Malformed JSON response in strict mode
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Err(OllamaUnreachable) with InvalidData IoError as source

userStories.notes: modifiesBehavior=true because this changes failure semantics for `recall --query` callers. Affected callers: `src/main.rs:440-471` (CLI dispatch), any test fixture that ran recall with Ollama unavailable expecting Ok(empty). Reuse `OllamaEmbedder` URL/model fields directly — don't re-derive from defaults. Pattern reference: existing graceful-degradation in vector.rs lines 79-89 (the code being replaced). Learning [1400]: The current vector backend explicitly returns empty results when Ollama is unavailable as a graceful-degradation pattern — this task converts that to opt-in via `strict` flag. Learning [1401]: Embedding config flows from ProjectConfig with Option<String> — preserve that flow.
qualityDimensions: Error message gives the user three concrete commands to fix the problem (ollama serve / ollama pull / --allow-degraded flag)
RetrievalBackend trait remains recall-policy-agnostic — no `allow_degraded` on RetrievalQuery
Empty query string short-circuits before any HTTP call regardless of strict mode
Backward compatibility: existing callers of recall_learnings() and CompositeBackend::default_backends() see no behavioral change
Strict mode opt-in via builder method, not construction-time required parameter
userStories.title: Failure-mode core: error variant, VectorBackend strict mode, --allow-degraded flag

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `LlamaBoxReranker::new("http://x", "model").rerank("q", candidates)` returns Ok with each ScoredLearning's relevance_score set to the cross-encoder score (NOT the original FTS5/cosine score).
Positive: results are sorted descending by relevance_score on success.
Negative: trait file does NOT contain a second `impl Reranker for ...` block. Trait + LlamaBoxReranker only.
Negative: no `unwrap()` or `expect()` in production paths of reranker/mod.rs.
Negative: documents sent over the wire never exceed 1024 chars per entry (byte length OK to be larger but char count ≤1024).
Unit test: empty candidates → Ok(vec![]) with no HTTP call (verify via mockito mock that the endpoint is never hit).
Unit test: response with index out of [0, len) range → Err containing 'out of range' (or equivalent specific message).
Unit test: response with duplicate indices → Err containing 'duplicate'.
Unit test: response with results.len() != documents.len() → Err containing 'length mismatch' or similar.
Unit test: malformed JSON response → Err.
Unit test: candidates content longer than 1024 chars → request body contains content truncated to ≤1024 chars (verify via mockito body matcher).
Unit test: candidates with non-ASCII content → truncate uses chars().take(N), no panic on UTF-8 boundary.

Known-bad: an impl that maps results via positional iteration (ignoring `index` field) gets caught by `test_orders_by_score` because the mock returns indices [2, 0, 1] (not in sorted positional order).
Failure mode: HTTP 5xx response → Err containing the status code.
Failure mode: connection timeout → Err with the underlying ureq error as source.
CONTRACT: `RerankResponse` struct field names match the llama-box `/v1/rerank` API: `results: Vec<RerankResult>`, `index: usize`, `relevance_score: f64`. Verify against gpustack/llama-box docs.
userStories.description:
Add a `Reranker` trait and the only production impl, `LlamaBoxReranker`, that calls llama-box's `/v1/rerank` endpoint. Strict response validation. Trait exists ONLY to enable test-double injection.

DO:
- Create `src/learnings/reranker/mod.rs` containing `pub trait Reranker { fn rerank(&self, query: &str, candidates: Vec<ScoredLearning>) -> TaskMgrResult<Vec<ScoredLearning>>; }`. Document in the trait doc-comment that the implementation MUST replace each ScoredLearning's `relevance_score` with the cross-encoder score and return them in descending score order.
- Implement `pub struct LlamaBoxReranker { base_url: String, model: String, agent: ureq::Agent }` with `new(base_url: &str, model: &str) -> Self`. Use the same connect=3s/read=30s timeout pattern as `OllamaEmbedder::new` at src/learnings/embeddings/mod.rs:36-44.
- POST to `{base_url}/v1/rerank` with JSON body `{"model": <model>, "query": <query>, "documents": [<title + content truncated to 1024 chars> for each candidate], "top_n": documents.len()}`.
- Document text format: build via `format!("{}\n\n{}", learning.title, learning.content)`, then truncate to 1024 chars using `super::super::commands::truncate_str` (re-export if needed) or inline a char-safe truncate.
- Parse response as `{"results": [{"index": usize, "relevance_score": f64}, ...]}`. Validate: HTTP 2xx; `results.len() == documents.len()`; every `index` in `0..documents.len()`; no duplicate indices.
- On any validation failure: return `Err(TaskMgrError::IoError(...))` whose message includes the URL and a snippet of the bad response (first 200 chars).
- Map response back: build `Vec<ScoredLearning>` where each entry's `relevance_score = response.results[i].relevance_score` (replacing the original retrieval score) and `match_reason = Some("cross-encoder rerank")`. Sort descending by score.
- Add `mockito = "1"` to `[dev-dependencies]` in Cargo.toml. Justify in a comment: 'Used by reranker HTTP tests.'
- Tests in `src/learnings/reranker/tests.rs` (or inline `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`):
  - `test_orders_by_score`: mockito returns `[{index:2,score:0.9},{index:0,score:0.5},{index:1,score:0.1}]` for 3 candidates → assert returned order is candidates[2], [0], [1] with relevance_score equal to the rerank scores.
  - `test_unreachable_returns_err`: backend pointing at `127.0.0.1:0` → assert `Err`.
  - `test_response_index_out_of_range_returns_err`: 3 docs in, response has `index: 5` → assert `Err` with message containing 'out of range'.
  - `test_response_duplicate_index_returns_err`: response has same index twice → assert `Err` with message containing 'duplicate'.
  - `test_response_length_mismatch_returns_err`: 3 docs in, 2 results out → assert `Err` with message containing 'length mismatch' or similar.
  - `test_malformed_json_returns_err`: mockito returns body `not json` with content-type text/plain → assert `Err`.
  - `test_document_text_format`: construct candidates with known title+content, capture mockito request body, assert documents[0] starts with title and contains content (or first N chars if truncated).
- Re-export from `src/learnings/mod.rs`: `pub use reranker::{Reranker, LlamaBoxReranker};`.

DO NOT:
- Add a second production impl. The trait exists for test injection only.
- Use `serde_json::Value` lookups for the response — define typed `RerankResponse` / `RerankResult` structs with `#[derive(Deserialize)]`.
- Send entire content unbounded — must truncate to ≤1024 chars to stay under the model's context.
- Swallow validation errors as `Ok(input)` — caller (FEAT-005) does the soft-fail; the reranker itself returns Err.

Edge cases:
- Empty candidates list: short-circuit, return `Ok(vec![])` without calling the HTTP endpoint.
- Single candidate: HTTP call still made, but result ordering is trivially correct.
- Candidate with empty title and empty content: send empty string as the document.
- Very long content (>10k chars): truncate to 1024 chars (char-safe, not byte-safe — must not split a UTF-8 codepoint).

Known-bad to guard against:
- An impl that returns `input.clone()` on parse failure would pass `test_orders_by_score` (because the mock returns valid data) but break the validation tests. Validation MUST return Err.
- An impl that uses `String::truncate` or byte-slicing for the 1024-char cap would panic on non-ASCII. Use `chars().take(1024).collect::<String>()` or similar.
- An impl that maps response indices via positional iteration (assuming response is already sorted by score) would silently produce wrong output if the server returned unsorted results. Must reorder by `index` field, not position.
edgeCases: Empty candidates: Ok(vec![]) without HTTP call
Single candidate: HTTP call made, trivial ordering
Empty title+content: send empty string as document
Content longer than 1024 chars: truncate char-safely
Non-ASCII content: chars().take(N), no UTF-8 boundary panic
Response with extra fields not in our struct: serde ignores by default; OK
HTTP 4xx with valid JSON body: still Err (response validation fails on non-2xx)

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Err(IoError) with descriptive message including URL
Err with status code in message

failureModes.cause: Response validation failure (index out of range, duplicate, length mismatch)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Err with specific failure category in message; first 200 chars of body included

userStories.notes: Reuse `truncate_str` pattern from src/commands/mod.rs:139 (it does char-safe truncation; check signature to confirm it does NOT append '...' or adjust call site). If it appends '...', use a local helper. Time agent: same pattern as `OllamaEmbedder::new` lines 36-44 — connect=3s, read=30s. Trait doc must explicitly state 'this trait exists for test-double injection only'. Reranker output: replace `ScoredLearning.relevance_score` with cross-encoder score. Set `match_reason = Some("cross-encoder rerank")` on each result.
qualityDimensions: Strict response validation: index range, no duplicates, length match, HTTP 2xx
Char-safe (not byte-safe) document truncation at 1024 chars
Typed deserialization (RerankResponse struct), not serde_json::Value lookups
Empty candidates short-circuit before HTTP call
Trait justification doc-comment says 'test-double injection only'
ScoredLearning.relevance_score replaced with cross-encoder score on success
userStories.title: Reranker module: trait + LlamaBoxReranker + mockito tests

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `RetrievalBackend` trait has new `retrieve_for_rerank` method with default delegating to `retrieve`.
Positive: `CompositeBackend::retrieve_for_rerank` calls each child backend's `retrieve_for_rerank` (verify via stub backends counting calls).
Positive: VectorBackend::retrieve_for_rerank includes candidates with score == 0.0.
Positive: VectorBackend::retrieve_for_rerank includes candidates with negative score.
Negative: `CompositeBackend::retrieve()` (the non-rerank path) is byte-for-byte identical to today's behavior — diff against baseline.
Negative: `retrieve_for_rerank` does NOT truncate to `query.limit`. The slate returned can exceed the limit; the recall pipeline truncates after reranking.
Unit test: stub backends A, B, C each returning {L1, L2}, {L2, L3}, {L4} → composite returns {L1, L2, L3, L4} with L2's score = max across A and B.
Unit test: store a cosine=0 embedding → `retrieve_for_rerank` returns it; `retrieve` does not.
Unit test: store a cosine=-0.5 embedding → `retrieve_for_rerank` returns it.
Unit test: default trait impl on FTS5/Patterns backend delegates to retrieve (verify by ensuring same results from both methods).
Known-bad: an impl that internally calls `self.retrieve` from `CompositeBackend::retrieve_for_rerank` would pass single-backend tests but fail the per-child counting test.
Failure mode: child backend errors propagate up.
CONTRACT: `ScoredLearning` struct field names (`learning`, `relevance_score`, `match_reason`) match what FEAT-002's reranker produces (verify by grep).
userStories.description:
Extend the RetrievalBackend trait so callers can request a candidate slate optimized for cross-encoder reranking — bypassing CompositeBackend's broken max-merge.

DO:
- Add a default-impl method to `RetrievalBackend` (src/learnings/retrieval/mod.rs):
  ```
  fn retrieve_for_rerank(&self, conn: &Connection, query: &RetrievalQuery) -> TaskMgrResult<Vec<ScoredLearning>> {
      self.retrieve(conn, query)
  }
  ```
  Default delegates to `retrieve()` so existing backends and tests are unaffected.
- Override `retrieve_for_rerank` on `CompositeBackend` (composite.rs): call EACH child backend's `retrieve_for_rerank` separately. Dedupe by learning ID across child results, keeping the highest score per learning. Concatenate `match_reason` strings (semicolon-separated) when same learning matched by multiple backends. **Do NOT apply the global max-merge sort or truncate.** The caller chooses slate size via `query.limit`. Return order: by descending dedup score (stable sort to preserve insertion order for ties).
- Override `retrieve_for_rerank` on `VectorBackend` (vector.rs): same logic as `retrieve()` BUT skip the `score > 0.0` filter at line 111. Cosine similarity in `[-1, 1]` is mapped via `score = sim * SCORE_SCALE`. Allow any finite score through so the cross-encoder can rescue moderate-similarity candidates.
- Do NOT override on `Fts5Backend` or `PatternsBackend` — default-impl (delegate to `retrieve`) is correct since their score scales aren't compromised by the same filter.
- Tests:
  - `test_default_retrieve_for_rerank_delegates`: ensure the trait default just calls retrieve (existing FTS5/patterns backends behave unchanged).
  - `test_composite_retrieve_for_rerank_unions_per_backend`: stub three backends each returning a different set of ScoredLearnings; assert composite returns deduped union with max-per-learning scores and concatenated match_reasons.
  - `test_composite_retrieve_for_rerank_no_max_merge_truncation`: assert returned count >= sum of per-backend counts minus duplicates (i.e. NO global truncation regardless of `query.limit`). The caller-side truncation lives in the recall pipeline (FEAT-005).
  - `test_vector_retrieve_for_rerank_keeps_zero_scores`: store an embedding orthogonal to the query (cosine ≈ 0, score = 0.0). `retrieve()` filters it out; `retrieve_for_rerank()` includes it.
  - `test_vector_retrieve_for_rerank_keeps_negative_scores`: store a NEGATIVE-similarity embedding (cosine = -0.5, score = -7.5). `retrieve_for_rerank` includes it (cross-encoder may still promote it).

DO NOT:
- Modify `CompositeBackend::retrieve()` (the non-rerank path). Keep today's max-merge for backward compat.
- Truncate inside `retrieve_for_rerank` — the recall pipeline does that AFTER reranking.
- Override on Fts5/Patterns — default delegation is correct.

Edge cases:
- A backend's `retrieve_for_rerank` returns Err: composite propagates the error. (Future: per-backend soft-fail could be added but out of scope here.)
- All backends return empty: composite returns Ok(empty); recall pipeline handles by skipping the rerank phase.
- Same learning matched by all 3 backends: deduped to 1 entry with max score and concatenated reasons.

Known-bad to guard against:
- An impl that calls `self.retrieve(...)` (the max-merged version) inside `CompositeBackend::retrieve_for_rerank` defeats the entire purpose. Test must verify per-child invocation by stubbing 3 children and asserting all 3 are called.
- An impl that filters cosine = 0.0 in `VectorBackend::retrieve_for_rerank` (copy-paste from retrieve()). Test must include a known cosine=0 case.
edgeCases: All backends empty: Ok(empty); recall pipeline detects and skips rerank
Same learning in all 3 backends: deduped to 1 entry, max score, concatenated reasons
Backend returns Err: composite propagates
Cosine == 0.0: included by retrieve_for_rerank (excluded by retrieve)
Cosine < 0 (negative): included by retrieve_for_rerank

failureModes.expectedBehavior: CompositeBackend::retrieve_for_rerank propagates the first error encountered

userStories.notes: Test stubs: implement `RetrievalBackend` on a small `MockBackend` struct that records calls and returns canned results. Useful pattern for FEAT-005 too. Composite dedup logic: use a HashMap<i64, ScoredLearning> keyed by learning ID; on collision, keep max score and concat match_reason. SCORE_SCALE = 15.0 in vector.rs:31; preserve. Reuse: `cosine_similarity` at src/learnings/embeddings/mod.rs:199-219.
qualityDimensions: Default trait impl preserves existing backend behavior (no overrides needed for FTS5/patterns)
Composite uses HashMap dedup, not nested loops (O(N) not O(N²))
VectorBackend keeps the SCORE_SCALE scaling but drops only the `> 0.0` filter
Stable sort within composite — equal scores preserve insertion order (matches existing retrieve() guarantee)
No truncation inside retrieve_for_rerank — pipeline-side concern
userStories.title: retrieve_for_rerank trait method + composite per-backend union + vector skip-zero override

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `ProjectConfig` deserializes JSON `{"rerankerUrl":"http://x","rerankerModel":"m","rerankerOverFetch":5}` correctly.
Positive: `resolved_reranker_config()` returns `Some((url, model, 5))` when all three set.
Positive: returns `Some((url, model, 3))` when over_fetch unset.
Negative: returns `None` (no panic) when neither url nor model set, no stderr output.
Negative: returns `None` when only one of url/model set, with stderr warning.
Negative: ProjectConfig::default() produces None for all three fields.
Unit test: each of the 6 helper test cases above.
Unit test: missing config file → defaults → resolved_reranker_config() returns None.
Known-bad: an impl that returns Some(default_url, model, 3) when only model is set would fail `test_resolved_reranker_config_only_model_set`.
Failure mode: malformed JSON in config.json → existing read_project_config falls back to defaults (existing behavior at line 75-78), reranker disabled.

CONTRACT: `RecallCmdParams.reranker_url` / `reranker_model` / `reranker_over_fetch` fields are Option types; main.rs unpacks the helper's tuple into these fields when Some, leaves None when helper returns None.
userStories.description:
Add the three new optional config keys to `ProjectConfig` and a helper that validates them, returning `Some((url, model, over_fetch))` only when both URL and model are set.

DO:
- In `src/loop_engine/project_config.rs:9-48`: add three optional fields (camelCase via existing `#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]`):
  - `reranker_url: Option<String>`
  - `reranker_model: Option<String>`
  - `reranker_over_fetch: Option<u32>`
- Add corresponding fields to `ProjectConfig::default()` (None, None, None).
- Add `pub fn resolved_reranker_config(&self) -> Option<(String, String, u32)>`:
  - Returns `Some((url, model, over_fetch))` when BOTH `reranker_url` AND `reranker_model` are `Some`.
  - Default `over_fetch` is 3 when unset; clamp to >=1 if set to 0 (eprintln warning).
  - When exactly one of url/model is set: print `eprintln!("\x1b[33m[warn]\x1b[0m rerankerUrl/rerankerModel: both must be set; reranker disabled")` and return None.
  - When neither is set: return None silently.
- Tests:
  - `test_resolved_reranker_config_both_set`: returns Some.
  - `test_resolved_reranker_config_only_url_set`: returns None and (test the helper at least; warning capture is best-effort).
  - `test_resolved_reranker_config_only_model_set`: returns None.
  - `test_resolved_reranker_config_neither_set`: returns None silently.
  - `test_resolved_reranker_config_default_over_fetch`: when over_fetch unset and both url/model set, returns 3.
  - `test_resolved_reranker_config_over_fetch_zero_clamped_to_one`: explicit 0 → 1 with warning.
- Add new fields to `RecallCmdParams` in src/commands/recall.rs:18-35:
  - `reranker_url: Option<String>`
  - `reranker_model: Option<String>`
  - `reranker_over_fetch: Option<u32>`
- Wire from `read_project_config` in main.rs:440-471: call `proj_config.resolved_reranker_config()` once and stash the result in RecallCmdParams (the three Option fields).

DO NOT:
- Make these fields required.
- Hardcode default url/model — they must be Option<String> so absence means 'reranker disabled'.
- Validate the URL syntactically (let llama-box error at runtime if URL is bogus; we already soft-fail on transport).

Edge cases:
- Config file doesn't exist: ProjectConfig::default(), all three None, reranker disabled.
- Config has rerankerUrl but no rerankerModel: warn, return None.
- Config has rerankerOverFetch=0: clamp to 1, warn.
- Config has rerankerOverFetch=999: pass through; the cap to 30 lives in FEAT-005's pipeline.

Known-bad to guard against:
- An impl that defaults reranker_url to 'http://localhost:8080' would silently enable the reranker for users who never opted in. Both keys MUST be Option with no default value.
- An impl that returns Some when only one key is set (treating the other as default) — see above.
edgeCases: Config file missing: defaults, reranker disabled silently
Both keys empty strings: returns Some('', '', 3) — let llama-box error at runtime
over_fetch = 0: clamp to 1 with warning
over_fetch unset: defaults to 3

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Existing fallback to defaults (line 75-78); reranker disabled

userStories.notes: ProjectConfig already uses `#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]` at line 11 — new fields just need snake_case Rust names. Pattern reference: existing `ollama_url` / `embedding_model` Option<String> handling. Default trait impl already covers None case at lines 50-62. The helper's stderr warning for the asymmetric case fires once at config-load time (before recall runs); no need to wire through verbose flag.

Asymmetric config (one key set) emits a clear warning and disables the reranker
over_fetch clamped to >=1 if set to 0
ProjectConfig::default() unchanged for users who never set these keys

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `recall --query 'x'` with reranker configured and llama-box mock up → results in mock-defined order, with each ScoredLearning's relevance_score replaced by cross-encoder score.
Positive: `recall --query 'x' --for-task TASK` with reranker → rerank applied first, UCB breaks ties within ±0.05 score band.
Positive: `recall --query 'x'` with reranker configured but llama-box DOWN → stderr warning printed, results returned in un-reranked order (NO error propagated, exit 0).
Positive: `recall --for-task TASK` (no query) → today's UCB pipeline; reranker NOT called even if configured.
Positive: CLI `--help` shows the new `--allow-degraded` flag and updated MATCHING BEHAVIOR section.
Positive: CLAUDE.md has a 'Reranker (optional)' subsection.
Negative: `recall --query ''` (empty) → no Ollama call, no reranker call.
Negative: `recall_learnings()` legacy entry signature unchanged — grep `pub fn recall_learnings` shows same signature.
Negative: reranker Err does NOT propagate to caller. Test asserts Ok and stderr capture.
Unit test: `test_over_fetch_caps_at_30` — limit=20, over_fetch=3, slate_limit=30.
Unit test: `test_over_fetch_min` — limit=5, over_fetch=3, slate_limit=15.
Unit test: `test_recall_query_and_for_task_rerank_then_ucb_tiebreak` — UCB only fires within ±0.05 bands.
Unit test: `test_recall_reranker_err_falls_back` — Err → un-reranked results + stderr warning.

Known-bad: an impl calling `backend.retrieve` instead of `backend.retrieve_for_rerank` would still pass `test_recall_query_only_applies_rerank` (because rerank still happens) but fail tests that depend on union-of-backends diversity. Add a test that the rerank slate includes a learning matched only by vector but with a low score (filtered by `retrieve` but kept by `retrieve_for_rerank`).
Failure mode: reranker times out → soft-fail (warn + un-reranked); recall exits 0.
Failure mode: rerank validation error (FEAT-002 returns Err) → soft-fail.
CONTRACT: `RecallParams.reranker` is `Option<Box<dyn Reranker + Send + Sync>>` (verify Send/Sync because RecallParams is passed across modules).
CONTRACT: `Reranker::rerank` (FEAT-002) replaces `relevance_score` with cross-encoder score — pipeline relies on this for `combine_scores` and ±0.05 banding.
userStories.description:
Wire the reranker into `retrieve_and_rank`. Apply only when query text is non-empty. Over-fetch from per-backend union, rerank, optionally UCB-tiebreak, truncate. Soft-fail with stderr warning on reranker errors.

DO:
- Modify `retrieve_and_rank` in src/learnings/recall/mod.rs:102-147:
  1. Build RetrievalQuery as today (lines 110-117).
  2. If `params.for_task` set, resolve task context (lines 119-126).
  3. **If reranker configured AND query.text is Some(non-empty)**:
     a. Compute slate_limit = `min(params.limit * over_fetch, 30)`. Inflate `query.limit = slate_limit`.
     b. Call `backend.retrieve_for_rerank(conn, &query)` (the per-backend union from FEAT-003).
     c. Call `reranker.rerank(query.text.as_deref().unwrap(), candidates)`.
     d. On `Err`: `eprintln!("\x1b[33m[warn]\x1b[0m reranker {url}: {err}; using un-reranked order")` and use the un-reranked candidates with their original scores.
     e. If `params.for_task.is_some()`: apply UCB tiebreak — group consecutive results within ±0.05 normalized rerank score; within each band sort by UCB descending. Use existing `ucb_for_learning` and `bandit::get_total_window_shows`.
     f. Truncate to `params.limit`. Return.
  4. **Else** (no reranker OR no query text): existing pipeline (`backend.retrieve` + UCB fallback + `rerank_with_ucb`).
- Pass the reranker through. Approach: extend `RecallParams` with `reranker: Option<Box<dyn Reranker + Send + Sync>>` and `reranker_over_fetch: u32` (default 3). Plumb from `RecallCmdParams::reranker_url`/`reranker_model`/`reranker_over_fetch` in src/commands/recall.rs:113-137 — when both url+model are Some, build `Some(Box::new(LlamaBoxReranker::new(url, model)))` and put on RecallParams.
- Update `recall_learnings_scored` (line 205) to accept the new RecallParams shape. Keep `recall_learnings()` (line 153) unchanged — it builds RecallParams without reranker.
- Update CLI after_help block at src/cli/commands.rs:440-467:
  - Document `--allow-degraded`.
  - Add a 'RERANKER' section: 'Set rerankerUrl + rerankerModel in .task-mgr/config.json to enable cross-encoder reranking via gpustack/llama-box. Optional rerankerOverFetch (default 3) controls candidate over-retrieval.'
  - Fix the inaccurate 'Results are ranked by last_applied_at' line — replace with: 'Results are ranked by relevance, with UCB exploration breaking ties within the same relevance tier (or within ±0.05 rerank score when a reranker is configured).'
- Update CLAUDE.md: add a 'Reranker (optional)' subsection under 'Embedding / Ollama Configuration' documenting:
  - The three config keys.
  - llama-box install pointer (https://github.com/gpustack/llama-box) and example invocation.
  - Soft-fail asymmetry: reranker = quality booster (warn + skip), Ollama = correctness primitive (hard-fail unless --allow-degraded).
  - `--query "X" --for-task Y` runs both rerank and UCB tiebreak.
  - Pointer to docker-compose at `docker/docker-compose.yml` (FEAT-006).
- Tests in src/commands/recall.rs:
  - `test_recall_skips_reranker_when_no_query_text`: --for-task only + reranker mock that panics if called → assert no call (verify panic doesn't fire).
  - `test_recall_skips_reranker_when_unconfigured`: no reranker on RecallParams → today's pipeline runs.
  - `test_recall_query_only_applies_rerank`: mock reranker returns deterministic order [2, 0, 1] → assert recall returns matching order.
  - `test_recall_query_and_for_task_rerank_then_ucb_tiebreak`: pre-populate UCB stats, mock reranker returns same score for two learnings → assert UCB stats break the tie.
  - `test_recall_reranker_err_falls_back_to_unreranked`: mock reranker returns Err → assert eprintln warning emitted (capture stderr) AND results returned in un-reranked order, no error to caller.
  - `test_over_fetch_caps_at_30`: limit=20, over_fetch=3 → effective slate=30 (not 60). Mock reranker assertion on candidates length.
  - `test_over_fetch_uses_min_with_30`: limit=5, over_fetch=3 → slate=15.

DO NOT:
- Run reranker when query text is empty or None — short-circuit at the top of the if branch.
- Truncate the slate before reranking — let `retrieve_for_rerank` return the full union, then rerank, then truncate.
- Convert reranker errors to recall errors — soft-fail behavior is mandatory.
- Use `recall_learnings()` legacy entry to plumb reranker — that entry stays unchanged (back-compat).

Edge cases:
- query text == "": no rerank (matches FEAT-001's empty-string short-circuit).
- limit == 0: existing code at line 107 normalizes to 5; over_fetch then runs against 5.
- over_fetch == 1: slate_limit == limit; rerank still runs but adds no headroom.
- backend.retrieve_for_rerank returns empty: skip rerank, return empty.
- All candidates have identical rerank scores: UCB tiebreak applies entirely within one band.

Known-bad to guard against:
- Calling `backend.retrieve` (the max-merge path) instead of `backend.retrieve_for_rerank` defeats FEAT-003. Test must inspect the call (e.g. via mock CompositeBackend or by asserting result diversity that only union provides).
- Forgetting to restore `query.limit` to params.limit after the inflated slate fetch — would cause double-truncation. Either don't mutate `query`, or save/restore.
- Using ±0.05 against raw rerank scores instead of normalized — rerank scores can be in (0, 1) for jina-v2 OR in (-inf, +inf) for some models. Normalize via min-max within the result set OR document the assumption that jina-v2 returns sigmoid-bounded scores and the band is in absolute units.
edgeCases: Empty query: no rerank, no Ollama call
limit=0: existing normalization to 5 applies, over_fetch on top
over_fetch=1: slate equals limit, rerank still runs
Empty candidate slate: skip rerank, return empty
All identical rerank scores: UCB tiebreak applies across whole band
--query + --for-task: rerank first, UCB tiebreak within bands

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Soft-fail: stderr warning + un-reranked results, exit 0
Soft-fail: stderr warning + un-reranked results, exit 0
Propagate Err — backend errors are not soft-failable (DB-level issues)

userStories.notes: modifiesBehavior=true because this changes recall ordering semantics for callers using --query when reranker is configured. Affected callers: src/main.rs:440-471 (CLI), any external consumer using `recall_learnings_scored`. Reuse: `combine_scores` and `ucb_for_learning` at recall/mod.rs:351-365. Stable sort within UCB band — Rust's `slice::sort_by` is stable. ±0.05 band: apply against the rerank score AS-RETURNED by the cross-encoder (jina-v2 returns sigmoid-bounded scores in (0, 1)). Document the assumption in a code comment. Soft-fail message format: `\x1b[33m[warn]\x1b[0m reranker <url>: <err>; using un-reranked order`. Capture stderr in tests via `gag` crate or by redirecting via a custom hook (check what the project already uses; otherwise log via `eprintln!` and don't assert on stderr text — assert behaviorally that results came back in original order).
qualityDimensions: Soft-fail on reranker errors: stderr warning + return un-reranked, never propagate Err
Empty query short-circuits before any rerank attempt
Over-fetch capped at 30 to bound llama-box load
UCB tiebreak only fires when both --query and --for-task are set
±0.05 band documented in code comment as 'jina-v2 sigmoid score'
recall_learnings() legacy entry signature unchanged
Stable sort within UCB tie bands
userStories.title: Recall pipeline integration: over-fetch + rerank + UCB tiebreak + soft-fail + docs

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `docker/ollama/Dockerfile` exists, FROM ollama/ollama, bakes the embedding model via ENV + RUN ollama pull pattern.
Positive: `docker/llama-box/Dockerfile` exists, FROM gpustack/llama-box, bakes jina-reranker-v2-base-multilingual GGUF.
Positive: `docker/docker-compose.yml` defaults to GPU, has `--profile cpu` for CPU-only.
Positive: `docker compose --profile cpu config` exits 0 (validates compose syntax) — verifiable in CI / locally.
Positive: `docker/README.md` documents both profiles with copy-pasteable commands.
Negative: compose default profile is NOT cpu — user must explicitly --profile cpu to disable GPU.
Negative: Dockerfiles do NOT pull models at container start; models are on-disk after build.
Negative: no `latest` Docker image tag used without a TODO comment about pinning.
Verify: built images launch successfully with `docker compose --profile cpu up -d --build` (manual smoke; document in PR description).
Verify: from host, `curl localhost:11434/api/tags` returns the baked embedding model in the list.

Verify: from host, `curl -X POST localhost:8080/v1/rerank -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"model":"jina-reranker-v2-base-multilingual","query":"test","documents":["a","b"],"top_n":2}'` returns a valid response.

Known-bad: a compose file with profile [gpu] on the GPU-only service (instead of [cpu] on the CPU-only variant) would mean the default `docker compose up` runs nothing — verify by ensuring `docker compose config` (no profile) shows BOTH services.
Failure mode: GPU host without Nvidia driver — compose up errors with a clear message (Docker's behavior; document in README).
Failure mode: build network failure on model download — RUN command retries; document timeout expectations in README.
CONTRACT: compose service ports match `ollamaUrl: http://localhost:11434` and `rerankerUrl: http://localhost:8080` — verify by grepping the example config in README.md against compose port mappings.
userStories.description:
Add `docker/` directory with Dockerfiles that bake the configured embedding and reranker models, plus a docker-compose file that defaults to Nvidia GPU and falls back to CPU via `--profile cpu`.

DO:
- Create `docker/ollama/Dockerfile`:
  - `FROM ollama/ollama:latest`
  - Bake the embedding model: `ENV OLLAMA_MODEL=hf.co/jinaai/jina-embeddings-v5-text-small-retrieval-GGUF:Q8_0`
  - Use a build script that starts ollama, runs `ollama pull $OLLAMA_MODEL`, then commits — typical pattern is a multi-stage build or a `RUN` that starts the daemon, pulls, and stops.
  - Concrete pattern: `RUN ollama serve & sleep 3 && ollama pull $OLLAMA_MODEL && pkill ollama`. Use `&& sleep 1` to let pkill complete.
  - `EXPOSE 11434`
  - Default `CMD ["serve"]` (inherited from base).
- Create `docker/llama-box/Dockerfile`:
  - `FROM ghcr.io/gpustack/llama-box:latest` (or pin to a specific version for reproducibility — leave a TODO comment with version pin guidance)
  - Bake the reranker model: download `gpustack/jina-reranker-v2-base-multilingual-GGUF` to a known path.
  - Use `huggingface-cli download` if the base image has it, OR `wget` from huggingface.co directly with retry.
  - `EXPOSE 8080`
  - `CMD` runs llama-box with the rerank flag and the baked model path: `["--rerank", "--model", "/models/jina-reranker-v2.gguf", "--port", "8080", "--host", "0.0.0.0"]` (verify exact arg shape from llama-box docs/help — link to https://github.com/gpustack/llama-box in the Dockerfile comment).
- Create `docker/docker-compose.yml`:
  - Two services: `ollama` and `llama-box`.
  - **Default profile = GPU**: each service has `deploy.resources.reservations.devices` block requesting Nvidia GPU.
  - **Optional CPU profile**: `profiles: [cpu]` on duplicated services (or use compose's profile mechanism) that omits the GPU reservation. Users run `docker compose up` for GPU (default) or `docker compose --profile cpu up` for CPU.
  - Healthchecks: both services have HTTP healthchecks against their respective endpoints (`/api/tags` for Ollama, `/v1/rerank` not idempotent — use `/health` if llama-box exposes one, else a simple TCP check via `nc -z`).
  - Network: shared bridge so task-mgr (on host) can reach both via published ports.
  - Ports: Ollama → 11434, llama-box → 8080.
  - Volumes: model cache shared via named volumes so rebuilds don't re-download. (Even with baked-in models, a volume helps with first-run resilience and lets the user pull additional models without rebuild.)
- Create `docker/README.md` (one screen):
  - `docker compose up -d` (GPU) — primary path
  - `docker compose --profile cpu up -d` — CPU fallback
  - Pointer to `.task-mgr/config.json` example: `{"version":1,"rerankerUrl":"http://localhost:8080","rerankerModel":"jina-reranker-v2-base-multilingual","ollamaUrl":"http://localhost:11434"}`
  - Note that the embedding model is baked at build time — change `OLLAMA_MODEL` ENV in `docker/ollama/Dockerfile` and rebuild to swap.
- Verification: `docker compose config --profile cpu` succeeds (validates compose syntax), and `docker compose --profile cpu up -d --build` succeeds in the verification step.

DO NOT:
- Use the `:latest` tag in production guidance — leave a TODO for pinning, but use latest for the initial PR to avoid stale-pin churn.
- Hardcode the embedding model in the compose file — keep it in the Dockerfile so swapping is one place.
- Run as root if the base image supports a non-root user — but don't fight Ollama's image conventions.
- Make GPU the silent default that fails on machines without Nvidia drivers — the CPU profile MUST be documented prominently.
- Add the Docker assets to CI yet (out of scope; users run locally).

Edge cases:
- User has no Nvidia driver: `docker compose up` fails fast with a clear error from compose. They run `--profile cpu`.
- User on Apple Silicon: GPU profile fails (no Nvidia); CPU profile works but is slow. README notes Mac users may prefer host-native ollama+llama-box for MPS.
- User on Linux without GPU: same as no-driver case.
- Model GGUF file size exceeds Docker layer limits: jina-reranker-v2 base is ~600MB, jina-embeddings-v5 small Q8_0 is ~150MB. Both well within layer limits. No action needed.
- Network failure during build: `RUN ollama pull` retries automatically; for llama-box's wget, add `--tries=3 --waitretry=10`.

Known-bad to guard against:
- A compose file that defaults to CPU (forgetting GPU as default) — user explicitly asked for GPU-default.
- A Dockerfile that pulls the model at runtime (defeats 'baked in' decision). Test: `docker run` the image without network access — embedding/rerank should still work because models are on-disk.
- A compose file that uses `command:` to override the Dockerfile CMD — instead, put CMD in the Dockerfile for portability across non-compose runtimes.
- A llama-box command line that points at a non-existent model file (typo in the path between Dockerfile bake and compose CMD) — verify by running `docker compose --profile cpu up` and checking llama-box logs for the model load.
edgeCases: No Nvidia driver: compose errors clearly; CPU profile workaround documented
Apple Silicon: GPU fails; CPU works slowly; README points at host-native alternative for MPS
Build network failure during model pull: retries documented
Different embedding model preferred: change ENV in Dockerfile and rebuild

failureModes.expectedBehavior: RUN retries; on persistent failure, build fails with descriptive error

failureModes.cause: GPU profile without Nvidia driver at runtime
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Compose up errors with Docker's standard 'GPU not available' message

userStories.notes: Use the SAME embedding model already in the codebase: `hf.co/jinaai/jina-embeddings-v5-text-small-retrieval-GGUF:Q8_0` (defined as DEFAULT_EMBEDDING_MODEL at src/learnings/embeddings/mod.rs:14-15). For reranker: `jina-reranker-v2-base-multilingual` from gpustack/jina-reranker-v2-base-multilingual-GGUF on huggingface. llama-box CLI flag exact format: check https://github.com/gpustack/llama-box README; reference shows `--rerank --model PATH --port N --host 0.0.0.0`. GPU profile syntax for Nvidia in compose: `deploy.resources.reservations.devices: [{driver: nvidia, count: 1, capabilities: [gpu]}]`. The user explicitly requested 'nvidia GPU required unless --profile cpu' — that's the default behavior.
qualityDimensions: Models baked into images at build time (verifiable: container runs without network)
GPU is the default; CPU is a documented opt-out via profile
compose syntax validates (`docker compose config` succeeds)
Healthchecks defined for both services
README has copy-pasteable commands and matches compose port mappings
userStories.title: Docker assets: Dockerfiles for Ollama + llama-box and docker-compose with GPU/CPU profiles

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: No code duplication that crosses 2+ call sites without justification
Positive: Functions in retrieve_and_rank kept under ~50 lines (the two-branch structure is acceptable but watch for nested complexity)
Positive: LlamaBoxReranker HTTP error handling consistent with OllamaEmbedder pattern (timeout + descriptive error)
Positive: Reranker trait has explicit 'test-double injection only' doc-comment
Positive: Code follows existing project patterns (ureq agents, ProjectConfig camelCase, Send + Sync trait objects)
Negative: No premature abstraction — additional Reranker impls or retrieval-policy types beyond what FEAT-001..006 require
Any issues found spawn REFACTOR-FIX-xxx tasks via `task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001`
userStories.description:
Review all implementation for DRY violations, complexity, coupling, and maintainability across the recall pipeline, reranker module, retrieval trait extension, config plumbing, and Docker assets.

DO: Check for duplicated logic between LlamaBoxReranker's HTTP error handling and OllamaEmbedder's; check function lengths in the new retrieve_and_rank flow; check that the Reranker trait justification doc-comment is present; check that the asymmetric soft-fail (reranker) vs hard-fail (Ollama) is documented in code, not just CLAUDE.md.
DO NOT: Skip — this gate catches issues before final review. Do NOT propose new abstractions just to look thorough; only flag duplication that crosses 2+ call sites with the same shape.
userStories.notes: If issues found: `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001` for each (priority 50-97) — atomic DB+JSON sync, no manual JSON edit. If no issues, invoke /simplify on any ugly touchpoint, then emit `<task-status>REFACTOR-001:done</task-status>` with a one-line progress note.
qualityDimensions: DRY across modules (ureq agent setup, error message formatting)
Single-responsibility functions
Pattern consistency with existing OllamaEmbedder, RetrievalBackend impls, ProjectConfig handling
No premature abstraction (Reranker trait stays single-impl in production)
userStories.title: Review for refactoring opportunities

Positive: All errors propagated with context (map_err, not unwrap)

userStories.description:
Review all implementation for quality, security, integration wiring, and documentation. RUN THE FULL QUALITY GATE (unscoped test suite).

DO: Check every new function for error handling, check callers still work, verify no dead code, check that CLAUDE.md and after_help doc reflect the shipped behavior, manually verify the behavior matrix end-to-end where possible. Run full cargo test (no scope filter), cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check.

Manual end-to-end smoke (document outcome in progress file):
- Stop Ollama, run `task-mgr recall --query 'x'` → expect non-zero exit + OllamaUnreachable hints.
- Same with `--allow-degraded` → expect exit 0.
- Start docker-compose with `--profile cpu` → expect both services up; verify `curl localhost:11434/api/tags` and `curl localhost:8080` (or appropriate llama-box endpoint).
- Configure rerankerUrl/rerankerModel, run `recall --query` with mock data → expect rerank applied (visible in --verbose output).
- Stop llama-box, same recall → expect stderr warning + un-reranked results.

DO NOT: Skip the integration wiring check. Do NOT scope the test suite — this is THE place the full suite runs.
userStories.notes: Spawn fixes via `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001` — DB + JSON synced atomically. End-to-end smoke MUST be documented (which steps ran, which were skipped if Docker is unavailable in the test environment). For mockito-based reranker tests, ensure stderr capture works without leaking warnings into other tests.

All new code wired to production entry point
Full suite green including pre-existing
End-to-end behavior matrix verified by smoke test (or smoke-test failure documented with reason)
CLAUDE.md + CLI after_help match shipped behavior
